title: Apprenticeship rotation board
summary: A skill-transfer board that prevents one expert from becoming the only person who can repair, cook, map, log, or manage water.
content: Long emergencies fail when knowledge is trapped in one person. The board lists critical tasks, trainer, learner, next practice, safety limit, and proof of competence. It values quiet repetition over heroic improvisation.
steps: List tasks that fail if one person disappears.
Assign at least one learner for each task.
Write the safety boundary before practice.
Record what the learner can do alone or with supervision.
warnings: Do not train dangerous electrical, gas, medical, structural, or chemical work beyond competence.
Do not shame slow learners. Treat this as a specific boundary while managing “Apprenticeship rotation board”.
Do not let training interrupt urgent safety needs.
common_mistakes: The expert stays too busy to teach.
Excluding people from suitable tasks because of age or disability.
alternatives: Run a smaller version of Apprenticeship rotation board with one responsible person, one helper, and one visible paper note.
Use a safer fallback location or method for Apprenticeship rotation board if the first choice becomes wet, crowded, contaminated, dark, unstable, or unsafe.
Pause Apprenticeship rotation board and verify the written record with a second person before moving scarce supplies.
failure_signs: People cannot explain who is responsible for Apprenticeship rotation board or when the next check happens.
Supplies connected to Apprenticeship rotation board are unlabelled, damp, contaminated, damaged, missing, or being used for the wrong purpose.
The routine ignores water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries and starts creating new risk instead of reducing existing risk during “Apprenticeship rotation board”
when_not_to_use: Do not use Apprenticeship rotation board as a reason to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, fire response, or official local instructions.
Do not use Apprenticeship rotation board when the location is structurally unsafe, chemically suspicious, smoky, flooded, violent, or otherwise immediately dangerous.
Do not improvise hazardous repairs, dosing, chemical handling, weapon use, or electrical/gas work under the label of Apprenticeship rotation board.
short_term: For the first hours of Apprenticeship rotation board, keep the action small, visible, and reversible: write the goal, assign one owner, protect vulnerable people, separate safe from unsafe supplies, and set a review time.
long_term: If Apprenticeship rotation board remains necessary for days or weeks, rotate roles, preserve written lessons, replace fragile supplies, and train another person so the group does not depend on one expert.
if_method_fails: If Apprenticeship rotation board fails, stop escalation, move to the safest fallback, record what failed, protect people first, and restart only with a simpler written plan.
environment_notes: Adapt Apprenticeship rotation board to the real environment: check water, hygiene, food contact, and waste boundaries, local weather, building condition, water access, light, noise, animals, neighbours, and safe exits before repeating the routine.
related_topics: Compost runoff and harvest hygiene boundary
